content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify real issues. The test uses a fixed 1-second sleep which is fragile under CI load — polling the weak reference is a strictly better approach. The result symlink is already in .gitignore but is tracked in the branch diff as a Nix store path change, which is unrelated noise.

## Votes

- `fr-8-52abe7e6`: Accept
  The test spawns a sleep 300 process, calls spawn_background_reap_with_timeout (5s timeout), then sleeps for exactly 1 second before asserting the Arc was dropped. The background task must SIGKILL the process group and waitpid — while this is typically fast on Linux, CI environments with overcommitted CPUs, containerized /proc, or cgroup freezer interactions can introduce scheduling delays beyond 1 second. The polling approach (option b) is clearly better: loop with ~50-100ms intervals checking weak.upgrade().is_none(), with a generous total timeout (e.g. 10s). This makes the test complete in ~100ms in the common case while tolerating 10x scheduling jitter, eliminating a real source of flakiness without adding baseline latency.
- `fr-8-b362cf8e`: Accept
  The git diff confirms the result symlink changed from one Nix store hash to another. Although result is listed in .gitignore, it appears to have been committed at some point and is now tracked — the diff shows a mode 120000 (symlink) change between master and HEAD. This is purely a build artifact reflecting whichever nix build was last run locally. It has no relation to the background-reap-timeout feature and should be excluded from the commit. Ideally the tracked entry should also be removed from git tracking (git rm --cached result) so .gitignore takes effect going forward.
